title: Architecture Intake — Runtime Detection Output
description: Output of qa-runtime-detector (§6 Step 1.7 Architecture Intake). Marker-bound classification of every runnable target (web/api/mobile/desktop/cli/library/worker) + how to start + health-probe each. NO-DOWNGRADE rule: no marker / high-confidence conflict / type-known-but-no-entry / only-low-confidence / host-incapable => decision=BLOCKED + blockers[], NEVER a silent default to web app. Written to .qa/evidence/<tag>/00-runtime.json. Consumed by qa-runtime-mismatch-gate.js (target kind vs produced E2E artifact type) and the test-plan synthesis.

decision.description: DETECTED only when at least one target is resolved with sufficient confidence; otherwise BLOCKED (no silent web-app default).

runtimes.description: Every runnable target. MAY be empty only when decision=BLOCKED.

framework.description: next / vite / express / fastify / nestjs / fastapi / django / gin / spring / aspnetcore / rails / expo / metro / …

markers.description: Provenance — every classification MUST cite the concrete files/signals that prove it.

signal.description: e.g. 'next dependency in package.json', 'next.config.js present', 'pubspec.yaml flutter:'

health_probe.description: URL (http://localhost:3000/api/health) or shell probe that returns 0 when ready

host_capable.description: Can THIS host actually run it? e.g. iOS simulator on Windows = false → NOT_APPLICABLE / RUNTIME_HOST_INCAPABLE downstream, never a fake PASS.

blockers.description: Non-empty when decision=BLOCKED. Each is a concrete reason the runtime cannot be resolved safely.

suggested_question.description: What to ask the user to unblock.
